Title: Comparative genomics of the medicinal plants Lonicera macranthoides and L. japonica provides insight into genus genome evolution and hederagenin-based saponin biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: cultivar collected from Longhui County, Hunan Province, China
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: cultivar collected from Longhui County, Hunan Province, China
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Extremely highly expressed in green floral buds of LM; much higher than in L. japonica; almost undetectable in LJ collinear gene
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450, CYP716A family, type II cytochrome oxidase
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Hederagenin-based saponin biosynthesis / triterpenoid saponin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream, oxidation step after β-amyrin formation and before downstream glycosylation/hederagenin-based saponin formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-28 oxidation of β-amyrin to form oleanolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Dried flower buds / green floral buds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal secondary metabolites with antiviral activity; α-hederin has reported therapeutic potential against COVID-19 by in silico study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes oleanolic acid formation and supports high accumulation of hederagenin-based saponins in L. macranthoides
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported; identified as a major candidate explaining differential saponin production between LM and LJ
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptomics; heterologous expression in yeast; in vitro enzyme assay with LC-QTOF-MS/MS; molecular docking; site-directed mutagenesis; truncation assay; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Comparison between L. macranthoides and L. japonica green floral buds / dried flower buds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LmOAS1 (EVM0008033) is extremely highly expressed in L. macranthoides buds but nearly undetectable in L. japonica. 2. Recombinant LmOAS1 converted β-amyrin to oleanolic acid by C-28 oxidation in yeast protein extracts. 3. Amino acids K145, P146, A148, L149 and R150 are important for activity, with L149G and R150N abolishing activity. 4. The N-terminal 1–26 aa transmembrane motif is required for catalytic activity. 5. LmOAS1 shows neighborhood replication/expansion to two copies in LM relative to one collinear copy in LJ.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Arabidopsis thaliana cytochrome P450 reductase ATR1/ART1 (heterologous redox partner in yeast assay)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful pathway gene for engineering oleanolic acid and downstream hederagenin-based saponin production in microbes or high-saponin honeysuckle cultivars; likely valuable for pathway stacking with β-amyrin synthase and downstream UGT genes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing BioProject PRJNA800599; transcriptome BioProject PRJNA825098; metabolomics by LC-QTOF-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expanded to two copies in LM (EVM0033113 and EVM0008033) on chromosome 5 versus one collinear gene Lj5A798G50 in LJ; promoter regulatory motifs differ between LM and LJ collinear genes; gene lies in an inversion-associated region discussed as potentially affecting expression

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: cultivar collected from Longhui County, Hunan Province, China
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: cultivar collected from Longhui County, Hunan Province, China
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Extremely highly expressed in green floral buds of LM; almost undetectable in LJ collinear gene
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase, UGT73 subfamily, contains PSPG motif
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Hederagenin-based saponin biosynthesis / triterpenoid saponin glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target intermediate toward terminal product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a rhamnose residue from UDP-rhamnose to cauloside A to form α-hederin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Dried flower buds / green floral buds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal secondary metabolites with antiviral activity; α-hederin is an intermediate with reported therapeutic potential against COVID-19 by in silico study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes α-hederin formation from cauloside A and supports biosynthesis of macranthoidin B-related hederagenin-based saponins
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key downstream determinant supported; one of the major candidate genes underlying high saponin production in LM
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptomics; recombinant protein purification from E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay with substrate/donor specificity tests; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; kinetic analysis; molecular docking; site-directed mutagenesis; LC-QTOF-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Comparison between L. macranthoides and L. japonica buds; transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves with cauloside A feeding
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LmUGT73P1 (EVM0012591) is highly expressed in L. macranthoides but nearly absent in L. japonica. 2. It specifically uses cauloside A as acceptor and UDP-Rha as donor to produce α-hederin; oleanolic acid and hederagenin were not accepted substrates. 3. In vivo transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana confirmed conversion of cauloside A to α-hederin. 4. The Km for cauloside A was 32.96 μM. 5. E83 is important for activity because E83A reduced catalytic activity. 6. LmUGT73P1 shows neighborhood replication/expansion to two copies in LM relative to one collinear copy in LJ.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for metabolic engineering of α-hederin and downstream hederagenin-based saponins in microbial or plant production platforms; useful in pathway stacking with upstream triterpene oxidation genes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing BioProject PRJNA800599; transcriptome BioProject PRJNA825098; metabolomics by LC-QTOF-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Renamed based on homology to Glycine max UGT73P2; expanded to two copies in LM (EVM0009409 and EVM0012591) on chromosome 4 versus one collinear gene Lj4C768T0 in LJ; promoter regulatory motifs differ between LM and LJ collinear genes
